L09.claim: Intruders tied me up and attacked my parents.
L09.shortExplanation: Pan arranged the attack and pretended to be another victim.
L09.fullTruth: Her father survived and contradicted her account. Evidence showed that Pan helped organize the attack, and her attempted-murder convictions remained in place.
L09.context: She described armed strangers entering the family home while claiming she had been restrained but spared.
L09.transcript: Intruders tied me up and attacked my parents.
L09.editorialBoundary: The verdict concerns Pan's claim that she was an uninvolved victim of unknown intruders.
L09.lesson: A story that explains why one person was spared should be tested against access, communications, and the surviving witness.

media.label: Open the complete police interview clip

L10.claim: It was not intentional. We were playing hide-and-seek.
L10.shortExplanation: Phone videos showed Boone taunting Jorge Torres while he begged to be released from the suitcase.
L10.fullTruth: A jury convicted Boone of second-degree murder. The recordings contradicted her initial claim that she simply forgot he was inside after a game.
L10.context: Boone said Torres climbed into a suitcase during a drunken game and that she believed he could escape.
L10.transcript: It was not intentional. We were playing hide-and-seek.
L10.editorialBoundary: The verdict applies to the accidental, harmless-game account—not every detail of the couple's relationship.
L10.lesson: Compare an explanation with evidence the speaker created before realizing it would be examined.

sources.title: Florida woman sentenced to life in suitcase murder

L14.shortExplanation: Lazarus had confronted Rasmussen and her DNA matched saliva from a bite mark on the victim.
L14.fullTruth: The investigation documented Lazarus's relationship with Rasmussen's husband, prior confrontations, and a DNA match. Lazarus was convicted of murder.
L14.context: Detectives invited Lazarus to discuss an old case without initially telling her she was the suspect.
L14.editorialBoundary: The verdict concerns the attempt to minimize prior knowledge and contact.
L14.lesson: Track whether a person's memory becomes more detailed only after investigators reveal what they know.

L19.claim: My parents went to their cabin for the weekend.
L19.shortExplanation: The cabin trip never happened. Halderson had killed and dismembered his parents.
L19.fullTruth: Digital records, recovered remains, tools, and location evidence dismantled the missing-trip story. A jury convicted him of both murders.
L19.context: Halderson reported his parents missing and supplied a detailed explanation for their absence.
L19.transcript: My parents went to their cabin for the weekend.
L19.editorialBoundary: The verdict applies to the claimed cabin trip and his professed ignorance of their whereabouts.
L19.lesson: Verify whether the destination, travel records, and communications in a missing-person story actually exist.

L25.claim: Two women kidnapped and held me captive.
L25.shortExplanation: Papini had stayed voluntarily with a former boyfriend and manufactured evidence of an abduction.
L25.fullTruth: DNA and phone evidence led investigators to the former boyfriend. Papini pleaded guilty to lying to federal agents and mail fraud.
L25.context: She repeated a detailed story about two masked female captors after disappearing for 22 days.
L25.transcript: Two women kidnapped and held me captive.
L25.editorialBoundary: The verdict concerns the two-woman kidnapping story that Papini admitted was false.
L25.lesson: Injuries can corroborate that harm occurred without proving the speaker's explanation for how it happened.

T17.claim: I survived nearly three days in an air pocket inside a sunken ship.
T17.shortExplanation: Divers found Okene alive about 60 hours after his tugboat sank to the ocean floor.
T17.fullTruth: He survived in a small air pocket inside the inverted vessel until saturation divers unexpectedly discovered him during a body-recovery operation.
T17.context: The rescue video looks staged because a hand suddenly reaches toward the diver from the darkness.
T17.transcript: I survived nearly three days in an air pocket inside a sunken ship.
T17.editorialBoundary: The exact timing is commonly reported as roughly 60 hours, not a full 72 hours.
T17.lesson: An extraordinary story becomes credible when the rescue itself is independently recorded.

T18.claim: I survived 71 days alone in the Australian outback.
T18.shortExplanation: Megee was found severely malnourished after surviving for more than two months in remote Northern Territory bushland.
T18.fullTruth: He said he ate frogs, lizards, leeches, and plants while sheltering near a dam before station workers found him.
T18.context: Parts of his account were debated, but his prolonged disappearance and extreme condition on rescue were documented.
T18.transcript: I survived 71 days alone in the Australian outback.
T18.editorialBoundary: The verdict supports the survival period and rescue, not every disputed detail about how he became stranded.
T18.lesson: Separate the well-documented core of a story from details that remain uncertain.

T21.claim: I fell from an airplane into the Amazon and survived.
T21.shortExplanation: Koepcke was the sole survivor of LANSA Flight 508 and then survived eleven days alone in the rainforest.
T21.fullTruth: Still strapped to a row of seats, the 17-year-old fell thousands of feet after the aircraft broke apart in a thunderstorm.
T21.context: Her survival combined the fall, crash injuries, and a long walk through the Amazon before she found help.
T21.transcript: I fell from an airplane into the Amazon and survived.
T21.editorialBoundary: The precise fall altitude is estimated; the crash, sole survival, and eleven-day rainforest ordeal are documented.
T21.lesson: Rare does not mean impossible. Look for a named event, physical injuries, and independent rescuers.

T23.claim: I survived a fall of more than 30,000 feet without a parachute.
T23.shortExplanation: Vulović survived the breakup of JAT Flight 367 and holds the recognized record for the highest fall survived without a parachute.
T23.fullTruth: She was trapped in part of the aircraft fuselage when it fell, suffering severe injuries but eventually recovering enough to walk again.
T23.context: The number sounds physically impossible, which is why the case remains one of aviation's strangest survivals.
T23.transcript: I survived a fall of more than 30,000 feet without a parachute.
T23.editorialBoundary: She fell inside wreckage rather than as an unprotected free-falling body.
T23.lesson: Check what a dramatic sentence leaves implicit; surviving a fall can include protection from wreckage and terrain.

sources.title: Highest fall survived without a parachute

T24.claim: I spent nearly 25 years in prison for my wife's murder, but DNA proved I was innocent.
T24.shortExplanation: DNA testing excluded Morton and identified another man connected to the crime.
T24.fullTruth: Morton was released after serving almost 25 years. The same DNA profile was linked to another murder, and the actual killer was later convicted.
T24.context: Morton's young son had described a different attacker, but the evidence was not properly disclosed at trial.
T24.transcript: I spent nearly 25 years in prison for my wife's murder, but DNA proved I was innocent.
T24.editorialBoundary: The exoneration rests on DNA and suppressed evidence, not merely Morton's consistent denial.
T24.lesson: A conviction is strong evidence, but it is not immune to later biological testing and disclosure failures.
